item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Stayed because of the image and the intro.

Upvoted for the narrative

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Than you for the +1!

ultimately harmless despite appearances

Incidentally, this is what makes Ikarana so unhappy. But hey, he can still dream.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the kind of SCP I like: utterly weird (a dreaming dildo…), but ultimately harmless despite appearances.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personnel requesting permission for testing with SCP-3883 must first submit a detailed experimental procedures log to the project supervisor

Someone fucked the scip dinnit they

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Belated reply, but yes, I believe so. Apparently Sly received it as a joke gift from friends. The natural response, of course, was to use it in an SCP article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is that a bad dragon dildo?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: IKARANA, CONSUMER OF THE DEEP, thanks you for your upvote and bids you interesting dreams.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just read an article about a sex toy that causes trippy dreams and it was… weirdly charming…? especially the ending, i hope poor ikarana gets his D-class personnel to play with.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And now there are at least two dildos on the SCP Foundation. yay.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: I was thinking about this for a while. While it doesn't really add much, it also doesn't take anything away from the article, and I supposed it could help get people to see and read about Omicron Rho or vice-versa. The stand-alone nature of the article means new readers could see that link and read more about wiki lore. I'm gonna give a tentative yes to this change.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Belated reply here, but I'm glad to read that you had an emotional response to this! Thanks for the upvote. :)

What were you smoking? Can I have some of that? If whatever it is made you come up with this concept, I definitely want some.

No smoking necessary! This is the product of some silly chat discussions, probably while lack of sleep was involved.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Hmm… I personally feel like this article is better off as a standalone for the humor aspect, but I will defer to my co-author here.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright. This one is mostly self-contained, but I did see one area a link could be added. Prior to having Dr. Harpy begin communication attempts with the object, you'd think the SCPF would have Oneiroi specialists of MTF Omnicron Rho give them a crash course in how to not get wrecked when dealing with dream entities. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

On 2/3/20██, Dr. Harpy, a researcher at Site-16 who specializes in Safe-class items, requested to attempt communication with SCP-3883-1. Request was approved. Upon awakening, Dr. Harpy described the dream, which was transcribed to create the following log.

To this:

On 2/3/20██, Dr. Harpy, a researcher at Site-16 who specializes in Safe-class items, requested to attempt communication with SCP-3883-1. Request was approved following Dr. Harpy's completion of a training course provided by Oneiroi specialists from MTF Omnicron Rho. Upon awakening, Dr. Harpy described the dream, which was transcribed to create the following log.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a sad sex toy.
And it made me sad too
+1

longer, more accurate expansion
– hide block

Allow me to reiterate, or perhaps, rephrase: That's a sex toy suffering from depression, body image issues and going through some kind of existential crisis.
And you made me want to hug it and tell it that it's going to be ok and that it's (he's?) very scary and I'm very afraid like in those wholesome tumblr posts that make me rage for no apparent reason.
You…
Made me…
A guy who's somewhat creeped out by sex toys in general…
Want to cuddle a green dildo.
And talk to it. And be afraid of it.
Well to be fair, I would be afraid of a dildo, assuming it approaches my ass, especially against my wishes and regardless of consnet.
But still, you made me to actively want to fear a stationary green dildo.
Just…
…
…
What were you smoking? Can I have some of that? If whatever it is made you come up with this concept, I definitely want some.

/noodle-armed dance of octopus victory

Thanks to everyone who liked the story of Ikarana and his dream to be more than the cards life dealt him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Silly, but in a good way. Sort of like the night light that summons Satan that reads children's stories.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stay classy~ ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the numbers that I wanted to use for an SCP I was working on. It is also a sexual SCP. I am disappointed I didn't get this number but I am happy to see something sexy in this very sexy number.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You made me feel bad for a sex toy… Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It just seems too blunt/direct for the Foundation rather than something along the lines of "phallic object intended for sexual penetration, or other sexual activity".

As someone who had to write too many psyc papers and research project proposals with a wordcount limit… I just went with the shorter term. Rest assured that there was an honest and awkward attempt to find a more professional way of stating it. We're totally open to other short wording suggestions here.

Otherwise, I really enjoyed it and I now feel a great sympathy for the plight of dildos.

Glad you enjoyed the read! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the opening description shouldn't use the word dildo. It just seems too blunt/direct for the Foundation rather than something along the lines of "phallic object intended for sexual penetration, or other sexual activity". Otherwise, I really enjoyed it and I now feel a great sympathy for the plight of dildos.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ikarana is a classy guy. Or at least he would like to be seen that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interviews are my favourite part of this SCP, just the picture of a octopus in an armchair its just……..its funny.

item.title: Re: 69+
item.content:encoded: Or we pretty much need to ignore this and make a pun? Maybe?

item.title: Re: 69+
item.content:encoded: Man, I need to find the chatlogs where we were hashing out the concept…
We've done it. 69 upvotes on a dildo scp. I want to thank everyone for reading and voting and Zyn for being my co-author! Hopefully you'll see more collabs from me in the future, it was fun!

item.title: Re: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
Aww, thanks for making the edit. I appreciate it. We're glad you enjoyed the article!

I wonder how many words you have to record for a GPS Voice Pack

Aside from the standard "turn", "left", "right", and so on words, probably just partial sounds. I had a GPS that would pronounce "canyon" as "can-eye-on" when it was part of a street name.

item.title: Re: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
Oh dang! Glad you liked it, and I apologize for not realizing you co-authored on it. Gonna go add you to the credit now!

(I wonder how many words you have to record for a GPS Voice Pack… Meanwhile, I would kill for a J.K. Simmons GPS)

item.title: Re: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
yaaaaaaaaay

Thanks for the audio adaptation, TheeSherm!

ETA: Your voice is very chipper and would be great for a GPS for short trips.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
Hey Sly, good to see you again! I made an adaptation of our favorite little Cthulhu wannabe. Loved the artice!

https://youtu.be/JzPNbwOQc2Y

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Footnote 2 notes the presence of an exception which is explained later on in the document, and footnote 7 mentions and gives additional information on the term "lucid dreaming". I think they're both worth keeping because in academic papers, the writer is not to assume that the (naive) reader knows everything ahead of time?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Footnotes 2 and 7 aren't really neccesary

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An entity haunting a sex toy who's in denial about being a sex toy. Wasn't expecting to read anything like this today!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"You're a sex to-"
"NO IM A BIG SCARY MONSTER!!!!"
I love this, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This makes me think of an older style of SCP writing, in a good way. It's a kind of off-the-wall sort of idea combined with a couple of very likeable characters.

I'm not sure what I expected when I clicked on the link to this article, but it definitely wasn't this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This provides a nice counterweight to all the articles that present dreams as extremely dangerous things, full of inexplicable terrors. It's a serious article about an entity that is embarrassed over its own lack of seriousness, and I can't help but enjoy that.

+1

item.title: Re: 3rd SCP, 2nd try!
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this was… this was something new for me. Definitely an entertaining sort of writing project, though. Sly initially asked me to read over the draft and see if it could be saved, and I just really liked the dialogue in the interviews, and the image of an octopus sitting in a chair being a gracious host.

and also i guess i'd never before considered the question, "what if a dildo dreamed itself into cthulhu because it was embarrassed by what it was?"

Anyway, time to clear my search history of all the scientific terminology research I did for this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an example of an elegant and, oddly, heartwarming skip that just so happens to be a sex toy. I'm pretty sure most downvotes last time just came from looking at the title instead of reading it. Glad to see it back up.

item.title: 3rd SCP, 2nd try!
item.content:encoded:
Here we go, 2nd time's the charm!

Image was taken by me.

Co-authored with Zyn after I self deleted due to downvotes. She helped me resize the image and clean up the clinical tone, she also came up with the reflection effect.

Thanks to Modern_Erasmus, MrWrong, and Kimse for feedback!
